text.tspan: Activation Function Impact on Tensor Distribution

ul.li: Asymmetric Policy: Mandatory for ReLU to prevent precision loss.
Symmetric Policy: Preferred for GELU/Tanh/SiLU to simplify integer arithmetic.
Clipping Threshold: Use 99.99th percentile for ReLU to mitigate the 0-spike bias.
Wasted Precision: Symmetric quantization would waste ~50% of the range (no negatives).
Zero-Point: Offset from zero required to map 0.0 to q_min.
Bimodal Stress: The spike at zero makes entropy-based calibration (KL) more volatile.
Range Utilization: Gaussian-like tail maps cleanly to signed INT8 range.
Hardware Efficiency: No zero-point subtraction logic needed in fused kernels.
